text:
PARIS 芒聙聰 At first glance, Andy Macdonald looks like his opponents at La Concorde skate park, whipping down into the bowl and flying above it, transitioning from body varial 540 to madonna to nollie kickflip. But then he takes off his helmet to reveal his gray hair.脗

That nollie kickflip? He invented it芒聙聰in 1993. Macdonald, 51, has been skateboarding since before all but one of his opponents inOlympic men芒聙聶s park skateboardingwere born.脗

芒聙聹He芒聙聶s got a minivan,芒聙聺 says Alex Sorgente, a 26-year-old representing Italy. 芒聙聹He芒聙聶s got three kids. He芒聙聶s such, like, a soccer dad. But he芒聙聶s out here competing against us. It芒聙聶s so rad.芒聙聺

Macdonald, who became the oldest Olympic skateboarder since the sport joined the program in Tokyo, finished 18th of 22 in the preliminaries in Wednesday芒聙聶s park event and did not advance to the final. But standing here in his "Team GB" dad hat, knee-length basketball shorts and sock tan, looking as if he says, 芒聙聹Looks like we got here just in time!芒聙聺 when a line forms behind him and 芒聙聹Time to rock and roll!芒聙聺 when he leaves a restaurant, he is thrilled.脗

芒聙聹People are like, 芒聙聵Are you going for the gold?芒聙聶芒聙聺 he says. 芒聙聹I芒聙聶m like, 芒聙聵If they芒聙聶re giving away gold medals for whoever has the most fun, I got it wrapped up!芒聙聺

He adds, 芒聙聹I was trying to represent, like, 芒聙聵Hey, this is fun, no matter what age you are.芒聙聶 This is, like, the coolest, funnest, most inclusive thing that you can do. And it芒聙聶s my livelihood. I芒聙聶m lucky enough that I can make a living doing it. But it keeps me young. Skateboarding is my fountain of youth. I芒聙聶ve been doing it since I was 12 years old. And I芒聙聶m 51 and still enjoying every minute of it.芒聙聺

Most of his peers have not even gotten to Ponce de Leon in history class yet. Coco Yoshizawa, 14, of Japan, won the women芒聙聶s street skateboarding event last week芒聙聰which made her the oldest Olympic champion in the history of the event, since in Tokyo, her countrywoman Momiji Nishiya, 13, took first place. The men tend to be a bit older芒聙聰the medalists in Macdonald芒聙聶s event were Keegan Palmer, 21, of Australia; Tom Schaar, 24, of the United States; and Sorgente芒聙聰but a quarter of the field need their parents芒聙聶 permission to see an R-rated movie.脗

Except for one. Dallas Oberholzer, 49, of South Africa, is Olympic skateboarding芒聙聶s other grandfather, and although he finished last on Wednesday, he too is having a great time. Macdonald is a legend of the sport who was last in Paris nearly two decades ago to do a demonstration at the Grand Palais with Tony Hawk, the most famous skateboarder ever; Oberholzer, who has no sponsors, rented out his apartment in Cape Town to finance his trips to Olympic qualifiers.脗

芒聙聹As an elder,芒聙聺 Oberholzer says, 芒聙聹I芒聙聶m here to look out over the younger generation, so I can report back, and I can approach these world skate guys and go, 芒聙聵Hey, you know what?芒聙聶 Because these young people are not going to have a voice, and I sure as s*** ain芒聙聶t scared of being a punk and having a voice.芒聙聺

As the sport芒聙聰which many skateboarders contend is more of an art芒聙聰becomes dominated by young kids, Oberholzer wants to maintain the culture that has given him so much. He sees it especially in opposition to sports such as soccer, where the Canadian women芒聙聶s team was recently hit with penalties for illegally filming opponents芒聙聶 practices.

芒聙聹We as skateboarders really need to come together芒聙聰as we do,芒聙聺 he says. 芒聙聹I think we rise above all the other sporting codes when you see how united we are. There芒聙聶s no spying on each other with drones. Andy Mac was giving me tips the other day because I was doing something wrong. We芒聙聶re helping each other, and we just want each other to have the best session possible.芒聙聺

That perhaps explains why Macdonald芒聙聶s fellow skateboarders were so thrilled when he qualified for the Olympics. He is American, but his father, Roderick, was born in Luton, England, which allowed Macdonald to take a shot at making the Great Britain team. He won 23 X Games gold medals in vertical, or vert, skateboarding, but since that is not an Olympic discipline, he competed in park. Because vert tends to take place on a wooden halfpipe and a fall gives you a chance to slide down a bit before you crash, it is much easier on the body than park, where everything is cement and you hit bottom immediately. Macdonald laughs when he sees teenagers wipe out and pop back up and try again, knowing a similar injury would have him out for a week.

芒聙聹When he made it, we were like, 芒聙聵F***, you deserve it,芒聙聶芒聙聺 says Danny Leon, 29, of Spain, with a laugh. (He is perhaps Macdonald芒聙聶s biggest fan, because when Macdonald is not around, Leon is the grandfather. 芒聙聹One girl asked me, 芒聙聵Are you married?芒聙聶芒聙聺 he says. 芒聙聹I was like, 芒聙聵No, I芒聙聶m not.芒聙聶 芒聙聵But you芒聙聶re 29!芒聙聶芒聙聺)

And they芒聙聶re all having fun. Macdonald is willing to take the jabs for his wardrobe (芒聙聹Colors [from the] 80s,芒聙聺 says Leon. 芒聙聹Everything now is black and white芒聙聺) and his equipment (芒聙聹The skull on his board graphic,芒聙聺 says Luigi Cini, 22, of Brazil. 芒聙聹Skulls are kind of, like, old-man stuff.芒聙聺), but there is one point he wishes to clarify: It芒聙聶s not a minivan. 芒聙聹I drive a Volkswagen camper van,芒聙聺 he says. 芒聙聹I drove it for 20-plus years. It was the beachgoer, taking the family camping and stuff. And I just finally sold it and got an electric car. So I芒聙聶m getting with the times.芒聙聺

Stephanie Apstein is a senior writer covering baseball and Olympic sports for Sports Illustrated, where she started as an intern in 2011 and has since covered a dozen World Series and three Olympics. She has twice won top honors from the Associated Press Sports Editors, and her work has been included in the Best American Sports Writing book series. She graduated from Trinity College with a bachelor芒聙聶s in French and Italian, and has a master芒聙聶s in journalism from Columbia University.

title: 51-Year-Old Skateboarder Andy Macdonald Is Having the Time of His Life at the Paris Olympics
description: In a sport typically dominated by teenagers, Great Britain芒聙聶s 芒聙聹rad dad芒聙聺 is having fun while also helping his next-generation competitors.
